questions.q: How do you want your club to win?
questions.sub: Pick the version of football that gets you off the couch.

options.label: Total control
options.text: Death by a thousand passes — starve the opponent of the ball until they break.

options.label: The knockout blow
options.text: Sit deep, absorb pressure, then hit them on the break before they know what happened.

options.label: Organized chaos
options.text: End-to-end, high-event, nobody's defense is safe. Entertainment over control.

options.label: Grind it out
options.text: Set pieces, duels, a back line that doesn't blink. Ugly wins still count.

questions.q: What actually makes you fall for a club's identity?
questions.sub: The football matters, but this is what you'll tell people at the bar.

options.label: Working-class roots
options.text: A ground built by factory workers, ultras who never sit down, a chip on its shoulder.

options.label: Galáctico glamour
options.text: Superstar signings, trophy cabinets that need their own wing, expectation as a baseline.

options.label: Homegrown pride
options.text: An academy that actually produces first-teamers, and a fanbase that notices.

options.label: Underdog with a plan
options.text: Overachieving on a mid-table budget, punching up, occasionally pulling off a miracle.

questions.q: Which World Cup storyline had you the most locked in?
questions.sub: This tells us more than you'd think.

options.label: The global superstar chasing history
options.text: One generational talent trying to will a whole nation forward.

options.label: The scrappy team shocking a giant
options.text: A roster nobody rated, playing above itself, ruining a favorite's tournament.

options.label: The kid who came out of nowhere
options.text: A teenager or unknown suddenly the best player on the pitch.

options.label: The manager's system working perfectly
options.text: A tactical idea executed so well it looked unfair.

questions.q: How do you feel about inheriting a rivalry?
questions.sub: Every club comes with enemies. Some are older than the club itself.

options.label: Give me the blood feud
options.text: I want a derby that shuts down a city, decades of grudges, songs about it.

options.label: A healthy rivalry is fine
options.text: Some needle, a fun weekend, nothing that ruins a friendship.

options.label: I don't care about rivalries
options.text: I'm here for good football, not a century-old grudge.

questions.q: Which region actually pulls at you?
questions.sub: Family, travel, players you admire — whatever the connection is.

options.label: British Isles
options.text: Rain, noise, a pub before kickoff.

options.label: Iberia (Spain/Portugal orbit)
options.text: Technical football and sun.

options.label: Italy
options.text: Tactics, tifo, tradition.

options.label: Germany / Central Europe
options.text: Terraces, atmosphere, club-run ownership.

options.label: France
options.text: Athleticism and star power.

options.label: Mexico & Latin America
options.text: Passion that doesn't come with an off-season.

options.label: My own city, USA
options.text: I want to be able to actually show up.

questions.q: Be honest about how you'll actually follow this club.

options.label: I want to go to games in person
options.text: Local, accessible, a season ticket is realistic.

options.label: I'm fine being a distance fan
options.text: 3am kickoffs, streaming, the full long-distance-relationship experience.

options.label: Doesn't matter, I just want the right club
options.text: Convenience is secondary to fit.

questions.q: Trophies or story — what matters more?
questions.sub: Last one. Then we stamp your passport.

options.label: I want to win. A lot.
options.text: Give me a club that expects a trophy every season.

options.label: I want a story worth telling
options.text: Heartbreak, comebacks, a multi-year arc — even if it ends in near-misses.

tag_labels.journey: a story worth telling over pure trophies
